channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Gauteng lawyer faces suspension for failing to pay client’s divorce settlement
item.description: A Gauteng-based lawyer, Fhatuwani Frederica Moila of Moila Attorneys, is facing suspension following a ruling by the Legal Practice Council (LPC) due to misconduct in handling a client’s divorce settl...

item.title: Man suffers heart attack after William Hill jackpot error
item.description: John Riding, a 76-year-old from Burnley, suffered a heart attack after being informed that his apparent £285,000 jackpot from William Hill was a technical error. Riding had initially believed he had w...

item.title: Middle East Tensions Push European Energy Prices Higher
item.description: Eurozone inflation has risen notably in March, reaching 2.5% compared to 1.9% in February, exceeding the European Central Bank's 2% target. This increase is largely driven by energy prices, which have...

item.title: Maguindanao Governor Offers P300,000 Reward for Info on Policemen Ambush Suspects
item.description: The governor of Maguindanao del Sur, Ali Midtimbang, has announced a reward of P300,000 for any information that could lead to the arrest of the gunmen responsible for ambushing and killing five polic...

item.title: NASA's Artemis II Crew Embarks on First Crewed Lunar Mission in Over 50 Years
item.description: NASA successfully launched its Artemis II mission, sending four astronauts on a journey around the Moon and back, marking the first crewed lunar flight in 53 years. The mission also serves as the firs...

item.title: Daughter Plays April Fools’ Prank on Father Claiming She’s Dating Elon Musk
item.description: On April Fools’ Day 2026, a Kenyan lady pulled a playful prank on her dad by claiming she was dating Elon Musk. She shared the conversation online, where her father’s reaction went viral. Initially, h...

item.title: Tanzania revises fuel prices amid global supply shocks caused by Middle East conflict
item.description: Motorists and fuel consumers in Tanzania are facing a sharp increase in prices as the Energy and Water Utilities Regulatory Authority (Ewura) adjusts cap prices for April 2026. Petrol is now selling a...

item.title: Northern Ireland authorities warn of ongoing threat from dissident republicans
item.description: Northern Ireland's Justice Minister, Naomi Long, has warned that dissident republican groups continue to pose a threat to the police, despite their reduced numbers. This follows a harrowing incident i...

item.title: Bus terminals adopt faster boarding system due to Holy Week travel rush
item.description: In preparation for the heavy influx of travelers this Holy Week, major bus terminals across the country are implementing a 'load and go' system. This approach allows buses to depart as soon as they ar...

item.title: Israel expands military presence in Southern Lebanon amid regional tensions
item.description: Israel has recently extended its military operations into Southern Lebanon, establishing a 30-kilometre occupation zone. This expansion comes as Israel faces conflicts on three fronts: Iran, Gaza, and...

item.title: Violence in Eastern Cape Follows Nigerian Community Ceremony for Igbo Leader
item.description: Protests broke out in KuGompo City, Eastern Cape, South Africa, after members of the Nigerian community installed Solomon Ogbonna Eziko, an Igbo leader, with the title 'Igwe Ndigbo Na East London' on ...

item.title: President Tinubu extends Prof. Bugaje’s tenure as NBTE Executive Secretary
item.description: President Bola Tinubu don officially renew Prof. Idris Bugaje appointment as Executive Secretary of the National Board for Technical Education (NBTE) for second and final five-year term. Dis renewal f...

item.title: Police arrest three suspects in Golders Green ambulance arson
item.description: The Metropolitan Police have arrested three additional individuals in connection with the arson attack on Hatzola ambulances in Golders Green, north London. Four ambulances belonging to the Jewish cha...

item.title: Orban Allegedly Uses Disinformation and AI to Influence Hungarian Election Amid Russia Ties
item.description: Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban faces a critical election on April 12, with analysts suggesting he has employed disinformation campaigns to shift blame for domestic issues onto Ukraine. Orban, k...

item.title: Luigi Mangione's state trial rescheduled to September, affecting federal trial timeline
item.description: In New York, the state trial of Luigi Mangione, accused of murdering a health insurance executive, has been postponed to 8th September, raising questions about the scheduling of his federal trial. Jus...

item.title: NAFDAC Reaffirms Commitment to Regulate Safe and Sustainable Herbal Products
item.description: The National Agency for Food and Drug Administration and Control (NAFDAC) don waka go front to make sure say all herbal products for Nigeria dey safe, effective, and of good quality. The DG, Mojisola ...

item.title: High court overturns rape conviction amid inconsistencies in complainant’s evidence
item.description: The Pretoria High Court last week overturned the rape conviction of Ekurhuleni businessman Pule Mokou, which was initially handed down by the Benoni Magistrate’s Court in February 2023. Judge FMM Reid...

item.title: Temeke Council to relocate traders from Tazama pipeline for safety and modern market development
item.description: Temeke Municipal Council in Dar es Salaam is moving ahead with plans to improve safety along the Tazama pipeline by relocating traders from hazardous zones. Temeke District Commissioner, Sixtus Mapund...

item.title: US Astronauts Depart for Landmark Lunar Mission
item.description: In a major milestone for space exploration, a team of US astronauts has successfully launched on a historic journey to the Moon. The mission marks the latest chapter in NASA's ongoing efforts to explo...

item.title: Canada’s Dunstone Secures Fifth Consecutive Win, Four Teams Tied at Top of World Curling Standings
item.description: At the world men’s curling championship, Canada’s skip Matt Dunstone led his team to a fifth consecutive victory, defeating Switzerland 8-7 in a tightly contested match during Wednesday morning’s draw...
